Transfer of FunctionsFor transfer of authorities, functions, personnel, and assets of the Coast Guard, including the authorities and functions of the Secretary of Transportation relating thereto, to the Department of Homeland Security, and for treatment of related references, see sections 468(b), 551(d), 552(d), and 557 of Title 6, Domestic Security, and the Department of Homeland Security Reorganization Plan of , as modified, set out as a note under .November 25, 2002section 542 of Title 6

children.text: upholding and defending the Constitution of the United States;
aiding and maintaining an adequate naval defense for the United States;
assisting the recruitment of the best personnel available for the United States Navy, United States Marine Corps, and United States Coast Guard;
providing for the welfare of the personnel who serve in the United States Navy, United States Marine Corps, and United States Coast Guard;
continuing to loyally serve the United States Navy, United States Marine Corps, and United States Coast Guard;
preserving the spirit of shipmanship by providing assistance to shipmates and their families; and
instilling love of the United States and its flag, and promoting soundness of mind and body, in the youth of the United States.

children.heading: .—Corporate Function
children.label: b .—Corporate Function
children.text: The corporation shall function as an educational, patriotic, civic, historical, and research organization under the laws of Delaware.
